paper_file: markdown/Small - 2024 - Yuan - Injectable GelMA Hydrogel Microspheres with Sustained Release of Platelet‐Rich Plasma for the.md
target_material_or_construct: PRP@GelMA hydrogel microspheres composite (PRP@GelMA-M) — an injectable biomaterial consisting of GelMA microspheres loaded with platelet-rich plasma (PRP), intended for intrauterine delivery to treat thin endometrium.
main_preparation_sections: Section 2.2.2: Preparation of PRP@GelMA Hydrogel Microspheres Composites (includes GelMA synthesis, GelMA solution preparation, microfluidic microsphere fabrication, PRP loading, and composite assembly)
evidence_distribution: All preparation steps are concentrated in sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.2 of the Experimental Section. Section 2.2.1 describes blood collection, anticoagulation, centrifugation, activation, and freeze-storage of PRP. Section 2.2.2 describes GelMA synthesis (gelatin dissolution, methacrylation, dialysis, filtration, lyophilization), GelMA precursor solution preparation, microfluidic droplet generation with oil/surfactant, UV curing, washing/centrifugation of microspheres, and final composite assembly with PRP-containing hydrogel.
branching_or_cycles: No explicit branching or cycles in the preparation workflow. Stages are serial: PRP preparation and GelMA synthesis are independent (parallelizable), then converge into precursor mixing, then microsphere fabrication, then composite assembly.
reason_if_incomplete_or_absent: N/A — complete protocol found with all essential preparation stages explicitly described.
scope_notes: Concentrations of PRP in the final hydrogel composite are described as 'different concentrations' without exact values in the protocol section. The paper provides sufficient detail for the core preparation workflow: PRP isolation, GelMA synthesis, microfluidic microsphere fabrication, and composite assembly.
